'''Adam Clune''' (born 8 June 1995) is a professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He was named as Giants captain for the 2026 Super League season<ref>https://www.giantsrl.com/article/9658/captain-clune-to-lead-the-giants</ref>

He previously played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

==Background==
Clune played his junior rugby league for the [[Junee Diesels]], [[Albion Park-Oak Flats Eagles]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=2020-06-22 |title=Dufty and Clune fulfillling primary school dream with Dragons |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/06/22/dufty-and-clune-fulfillling-primary-school-dream-with-dragons/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200623162056/https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/06/22/dufty-and-clune-fulfillling-primary-school-dream-with-dragons/ |archive-date=2020-06-23 |access-date=2020-08-20 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}</ref> and [[Western Suburbs Red Devils]].

==Playing career==
===2020===
Clune made his debut in round 4 of the [[2020 NRL season]] for [[St. George Illawarra]] against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-06-02 |title=Round 4 NRL team lists |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/06/02/round-4-nrl-squad-announcements/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200603125302/https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/06/02/round-4-nrl-squad-announcements/ |archive-date=2020-06-03 |access-date=2020-06-02 |website=NRL.com}}</ref>

He played 15 games for the club in his debut season as they finished 13th on the table and missed out on the finals.<ref>{{Cite news |last=O'Loughlin |first=Liam |date=2020-10-26 |title=NRL 2020 Season Review: How will your side fare next year? |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201026175328/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |archive-date=2020-10-26}}</ref>

===2021===
Clune scored his first NRL try in round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]] against local rivals [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=2021-03-14 |title=Wet weather lessons learned as Sharks too slick for Dragons |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/03/14/rivals-fire-up-but-sharks-too-slick-in-wet-for-dragons/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210314091442/https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/03/14/rivals-fire-up-but-sharks-too-slick-in-wet-for-dragons/ |archive-date=2021-03-14 |access-date=2021-03-16 |website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

In August, he signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2022.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-30 |title=Adam Clune inks Knights contract |url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2021/08/27/adam-clune-inks-knights-contract/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210830065434/https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2021/08/27/adam-clune-inks-knights-contract/ |archive-date=2021-08-30 |access-date=2021-08-30 |website=[[Newcastle Knights]]}}</ref>

Clune played a total of 10 matches for [[St. George Illawarra]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished 11th on the table and missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-st-george-illawarra-dragons-vs-north-queensland-cowboys-live-stream-blog-score-updates-supercoach/news-story/bb4205d15445f75b33404ef10c54a3b6/|title=Luckless Cowboy ends 14-game losing streak; Dragons find their new No.6: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=28 August 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
Clune played a total of 18 matches for Newcastle in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 14th on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-newcastle-knights-season-review/zuanch2lkdlbojvv3rtaafcg/|title=NRL 2022: Newcastle Knights season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=31 August 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
In 2023, after spending most of the year in the [[NSW Cup]], Clune was called into the Knights NRL team for the last three weeks of the season, replacing [[Jackson Hastings]] due to injury. Clune played in the Knights' 10–40 loss to [[New Zealand Warriors]] in week two of the finals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMjM2MCwyMzg3LDI0NTUsMjQ1Ni0tNC0tLS0tMjk4MzEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - RLP|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> He parted ways with the club at the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/09/05/kalyn-ponga-claims-2023-danny-buderus-medal/|title=Kalyn Ponga claims 2023 Danny Buderus Medal|date=5 September 2023|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

On 26 October, it was reported that he had joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] for 2024 on a three-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=26 Oct 2023|title=Adam Clune: Huddersfield Giants sign Australian scrum-half on three-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/67223555.amp}}</ref>

===2024===
Clune played 26 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season as the club had a difficult campaign finishing 9th on the table, despite this clune had 8 assists and 4 tries to record his most consistent season to date.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===2025===
Clune played only six matches for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
